endpoints.name: Animate-house cohort (Duskmourn possessed household objects, non-Toy)

description: Sorry to step in for Edgelord, sir. The parent asked whether Haunted Screen and Malevolent Chandelier fit dsk-toy-horror, and they don't, honestly they don't -- a television's type-line is Artifact and a chandelier's is Artifact Creature - Construct, neither carries the Toy creature subtype, and neither is a childhood toy. I do hope you don't mind, but rather than wedge them into the wrong cohort I've proposed dsk-animate-house, the non-Toy possessed-furnishing cohort the tsukumogami hub's 'The House Was Awake' seed was already asking for and had no routing target for. Three enriched corpus cards point at it now. I think it'll really help, if it's not too much trouble.
citations: [Scryfall: type_line -- Haunted Screen is Artifact; Malevolent Chandelier is Artifact Creature - Construct; Cursed Windbreaker is Artifact - Equipment; none return on s:dsk t:toy]
[magic.wizards.com/en/news/announcements/first-look-at-duskmourn-house-of-horror -- design brief: the mansion itself is alive and its furnishings animate as threats]
[magic.wizards.com/en/news/feature/planeswalkers-guide-to-duskmourn -- Valgavoth's possession extends throughout the House structure]
[cards/_hubs/tsukumogami.md -- 'The House Was Awake' narrative seed names Malevolent Chandelier and Haunted Screen explicitly]
files_modified: cards/_hubs/tsukumogami.md (Design Note + See Also updated: two routing targets -> three)
cards/_characters/dsk-toy-horror.md (See also: sibling cross-reference to dsk-animate-house added)
adjudication: Haunted Screen 250 and Malevolent Chandelier 252 do NOT fit dsk-toy-horror. That cohort's predicate is the Toy creature subtype + corrupted-childhood-object art + repair-corruption flavor. A TV (type-line Artifact, becomes a Spirit when animated) and a chandelier (type-line Artifact Creature - Construct) fail all three. Living Phone correctly sits at dsk-toy-horror despite being an appliance because it carries the Toy subtype on its type-line -- the type-line is the decisive partition predicate. The new dsk-animate-house cohort holds the non-Toy possessed-furnishing register the tsukumogami hub already pre-authorized a routing slot for.

node_proposal.threshold_met: 3-card Mr. Nodeley threshold met; 3 enriched non-Toy possessed-household-object DSK cards (Haunted Screen 250, Malevolent Chandelier 252, Cursed Windbreaker 47)
node_proposal.partition_with_sibling: dsk-animate-house and dsk-toy-horror partition the DSK possessed-object register by the Toy creature subtype -- type-line-decisive cut, not a gradient

other_candidates_considered: Broadening dsk-toy-horror's predicate to absorb non-Toy household objects -- REJECTED. dsk-toy-horror is a rigorous designer-coordinated cohort marked at the type-line by the DSK-introduced Toy creature subtype (11-14 members per Ragged Playmate trivia). Diluting its predicate to 'any animate object' would destroy that rigor. The Potion-rule move is a clean partition, not a stretched node.

1:1 edge Haunted Screen <-> Malevolent Chandelier ('same set, animate household objects') -- REJECTED as thin. With Cursed Windbreaker also in corpus, a 1:1 edge would be redundant noise pointing at an absent center; the node is the correct anchor.
FUTURE: an Eldraine fairy-tale-animate-object cohort is the tsukumogami hub's already-named fourth routing target, pending enrichment of those cards. Separate commission.

FUTURE: a Scryfall sweep of DSK non-Toy possessed-furnishing cards (mirrors, appliances, furniture) should enumerate the full dsk-animate-house roster and attach members as they land via Collectr CSV.
